questions.question: What is a class in Java?
options: A running instance of a program
A blueprint or template for creating objects
A method that returns a value
A variable that stores multiple values
questions.answer: A blueprint or template for creating objects

questions.question: What is an object in Java?
options: A synonym for a class
A file that contains Java code
A specific instance created from a class
A keyword used to define methods
questions.answer: A specific instance created from a class

questions.question: Which keyword is used to create a new object in Java?
options: create
object
this
new
questions.answer: new

questions.question: What are instance variables in a class also called?
options: Methods
Fields
Parameters
Constants
questions.answer: Fields

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four pillars of Object-Oriented Programming?
options: Encapsulation
Compilation
Inheritance
Polymorphism
questions.answer: Compilation

questions.question: What does encapsulation mean in OOP?
options: The ability of a class to inherit from multiple parent classes
Bundling data and the methods that operate on that data together, while restricting direct access
Running the same method in different ways
Hiding the implementation of an entire program from the user
questions.answer: Bundling data and the methods that operate on that data together, while restricting direct access

questions.question: What does inheritance mean in OOP?
options: A class can acquire the fields and methods of another class
A class can only be instantiated once
Objects must share the same data values
Methods can only be defined in the main class
questions.answer: A class can acquire the fields and methods of another class

questions.question: What does polymorphism mean in OOP?
options: Each object must have a unique class
A program can only use one data type at a time
The ability for objects of different classes to respond to the same method call in different ways
Variables can only store one type of data
questions.answer: The ability for objects of different classes to respond to the same method call in different ways

questions.question: What does abstraction mean in OOP?
options: Making all fields and methods public
Showing only essential details and hiding the complex implementation
Creating as many objects as possible from one class
Writing code without any comments
questions.answer: Showing only essential details and hiding the complex implementation

options: Creates a new Dog class called Buddy
Declares a local variable named Buddy
Assigns the value "Buddy" to the name field of the myDog object
Calls the name method on the myDog object
questions.answer: Assigns the value "Buddy" to the name field of the myDog object

questions.question: What is a constructor in Java?
options: A method that deletes an object from memory
A special method that is called when a new object is created
A keyword used to import other classes
A field that stores the name of the class
questions.answer: A special method that is called when a new object is created

questions.question: How many objects can you create from a single class?
options: Only one
Exactly two
A maximum of ten
As many as needed
questions.answer: As many as needed

questions.question: Which operator is used to access fields and methods of an object?
options: The double colon (::)
The arrow (->)
The dot (.)
The hash (#)
questions.answer: The dot (.)

options: A method
A constructor
A field (instance variable)
A class name
questions.answer: A field (instance variable)

options: This is a long book.
This is a short read.
Nothing is printed
questions.answer: This is a short read.

questions.question: If two objects are created from the same class, what is true about their fields?
options: They must always have the same values
Each object holds its own independent copy of the fields
Changing one object's field automatically changes the other's
Only the first object created can have field values assigned
questions.answer: Each object holds its own independent copy of the fields

questions.question: What is the difference between a field and a method?
options: A field stores data; a method performs an action
A field performs an action; a method stores data
Fields and methods are the same thing
A field is only used in the main method; a method is used in all classes
questions.answer: A field stores data; a method performs an action

questions.question: Which of the following correctly creates a Student object?
options: Student s1 = Student();
new Student s1 = Student();
Student s1 = new Student();
Student s1 = create Student();
questions.answer: Student s1 = new Student();

questions.question: In the Dog class example, what does the bark() method do?
options: Returns the dog's name as a String
Sets the dog's name field
Prints the dog's name followed by 'says: Woof!'
Creates a new Dog object
questions.answer: Prints the dog's name followed by 'says: Woof!'

questions.question: Which OOP pillar is most directly demonstrated by creating a class with fields and methods that operate on those fields?
options: Polymorphism
Inheritance
Abstraction
Encapsulation
questions.answer: Encapsulation

questions.question: What is the return type of a method declared as 'public void describe()'?
options: String
int
It does not return a value
boolean
questions.answer: It does not return a value

questions.question: Which of the following is true about Java as an OOP language?
options: Java does not support classes
Java uses classes as blueprints to create objects
Java only allows one object per program
Java does not support methods inside classes
questions.answer: Java uses classes as blueprints to create objects

options: The student is in good standing.
The student's GPA is 3.8.
The student is on the honor roll.
Nothing is printed because gpa is a double.
questions.answer: The student is on the honor roll.

questions.question: Where must the main method be placed in order to run a Java program that uses a separate class like Book?
options: Inside the Book class as a regular method
In a separate class such as BookMain with the signature 'public static void main(String[] args)'
The main method is not needed when using objects
Inside every method of the Book class
questions.answer: In a separate class such as BookMain with the signature 'public static void main(String[] args)'

questions.question: Why is Object-Oriented Programming a popular approach to software development?
options: It forces all code to be written in a single file
It eliminates the need for variables
It organizes code into reusable, modular structures that model real-world entities
It makes programs run faster than any other paradigm
questions.answer: It organizes code into reusable, modular structures that model real-world entities
